props.caption: Europe finances the drone as an industrial base, from public loans to venture capital

items.note: Russia hit the capital with roughly 570 aerial weapons overnight — 74 missiles and nearly 500 drones — killing at least 21 people as air defence downed about 92% of the salvo. Kyiv logged damage at more than 20 sites, most of them residential; Moscow claimed military-industrial targets.

Qatari and Pakistani mediators reported positive but limited progress in indirect US-Iran technical talks, with the next round unscheduled. Hormuz kept flowing and Brent slid to a four-month low near $71 as the war premium drained, while IAEA inspectors have still not returned to the struck nuclear sites.

Seoul steered more than $576bn toward power-rich land and domestic chips as Washington opened 16 federal sites for AI datacenters, while China's low-cost open-weight models narrowed the gap. Semiconductor stocks fell on 2 July as investors weighed the buildout against cheaper inference.

Rapid Support Forces drone strikes hit El Obeid in North Kordofan as the army clawed back ground in West Darfur and Blue Nile. The mass-atrocity risk around the city stayed live while global attention held elsewhere.

items.what: Serbia's President Vučić said he would resign within weeks and call early elections, after 18 months of student-led protests; demonstrators doubted he would fully cede power.
The US declined to renew USMCA in its current form, USTR said, keeping the pact in force but pushing it into rolling annual reviews; Washington and Mexico meet again the week of 20 July.
Météo-France called 24-25 June the hottest days on record at country scale, and England logged its warmest June ever, as heat curbed French nuclear output and lifted wholesale power prices.
Afghanistan said it struck into Pakistan and Islamabad said it shot down four drones from Afghan territory, extending a cross-border cycle no mediator has yet contained.
An AtlasIntel poll put Lula ahead of Senator Flavio Bolsonaro 48.8% to 42.3% in a 2026 runoff, as a family rupture threatened the Brazilian right's standing with women voters.
South Korea's June exports posted their strongest growth in decades, led by a surge in chip shipments, as the AI-hardware boom showed up in a whole country's trade data.
